title: Which is more difficult to pilot Eva or Gundam
display_title: Which is more difficult to pilot Eva or Gundam
wikitext:
[]: Which is more difficult to pilot? Eva or Gundam?             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Oct 11, 2009 11:07 pm Just had to pose this question here. So which one do you guys think would be more difficult to learn how to pilot? I heard from some mods here a month or two ago that Eva pilots need to deal with up to 1000 or more control schemes/operational maneuvers/steps/data etc.... Complicated to be more or less straight. But if its that difficult to pilot an Eva how did Shinji ever learn how to pilot it during the short intervals between battling Sachiel and Shamshel? I know Evas have that who sychro ratio thing going for them. And pilots seem to have both analog and mind control technology to assist them in piloting their bio-mechs. And of course they have that added benefit or disadvantage depending on how you look at it...with their Evas being possessed by a resident spirit or soul. Man Evas are haunted i guess. As for Gundams....piloting is done mostly through analog i think. Pressing buttons...moving a joystick and pushing pedals and levers. There are so many variations of the mobile suit though... I'm not a big gundam fan but i have heard that in some gundam eps in a particular series young student pilots would actually train in an academy to pilot a gundam. Think Gunbuster....with the space academy in Okinawa. So yeah i pose this question out for you guys. Which one is harder to pilot? An Eva or a Gundam? Discuss.

[]:             Quote Post by Alaska Slim » Sun Oct 11, 2009 11:17 pm I think this should go in Eva Chit-chat, not here.

[]:             Quote Post by Eva Yojimbo » Sun Oct 11, 2009 11:25 pm Just because of the "ewww" factor in piloting a bio-mecha with the soul of your mother I think I'd have to go with Evas. Plus there's that whole wonky "synch" thing.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Sun Oct 11, 2009 11:28 pm Alaska Slim wrote: I think this should go in Eva Chit-chat, not here.

[]:             Quote Post by Otakon 08 Ikari » Sun Oct 11, 2009 11:38 pm It depends on what Gundam we are talking about and are you a NEWTYPE or not? I would believe that piloting a Gundam is harder since you have to control more physically.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryjin » Mon Oct 12, 2009 1:43 am Hmmmm..... while I'm not the biggest gundam fan, I do know a little about the show and in it mobile suits are pretty common and are used in much the same way as tanks are used today. So I would think they'd have to be somewhat easy to learn how to pilot, probably a little harder to use then a tank but no where near as hard as an jet fighter. Also given there doesn't seem to be any waldo gloves in the cockpit (waldo gloves are a force feed back devise that fits over the hands allowing a user to "handle" digital objects, in this case they would be tied into the arms control allowing them to mimic the pilots actions) I would figure that a lot of the more complex options are handled by prewritten programing, to give you an example of this, when a mobile suit is given a weapon the computer anlize it 's shape and weight and terrain its on and with this data commands are sent to the arms, legs and possibly to hydraulics in the torso to assume a stance that would best accommodate the weapon, the pilot in this case wouldn't need to impute any commands to carry this out. When the pilot wants to fire they simple move the targeter over the target, sensors then scan and lock on to it, a ballistic program is run to figure the optimum firing angle and commands are sent to the suits body moving its legs, torso and arms in the best possible way to intercept the target based on accuracy of the senor data. Of course this is just me fanwanking an answer, the actual truth behind how gundams are piloted is they operate on shear awesome. As for eva's while there are some analog commands involved, for the most part the an eva is piloted by becoming synchronize with the pilots brain. In this case lets say a eva pilot wants there eva to walk, a gundam pilot in this case would step on a peddle or push a stick forward which would start a chain of commands starting with various sensors reading what type of terrain there on, how stable it is, level of incline, the air speed and pressure, how heavy is the suits load out and its center of gravity, this information then sent to a computer where a bunch of complex geometry is computed figuring the best way to take then next step which is then sent to the to the various parts of the gundam, the eva pilot just thinks about walking, a nerve pule is detected by the eva which then starts a complected chain of events which is handled subconsciously, nerves in the evas foot detect what type of terrain there on, how stable it is and its level of incline, nerves across its body detect wind speed and how heavy of load out it has , it's inner ear senses air pressures and center of gravity, its eyes pick up visual information which also helps with determining what kind of ground it's on in conjugation with it's feet data, this data is compared to previous experience stored in the evas muscle memory as well as the pilots actual memory then this info is then sent to the pilots Cerebellum which does a bunch of geometry majick computing the best way to take a step which is then sent to the evas various body parts. Now from this comparison it would seem that the eva and gundam are a pretty even match, with them both having analogue controls with the more complex operations being handled of screen, a data processor for a gundam and the pilots brain for the eva, though some may argue that the fact that instead of having to hit a button to move, an eva pilot only has to think to move may give then the edge, a counter point can be bought up that muscle memory, once a pilot has gain enough, reacts faster then one can think (but in practice this is probably a moot point however as it in the end comes down to personal preface with some finding it easier to think something out while other find developing muscle memory easier), which again equals them out, there are 2 things that work in the gundams favor if we look a little more closely. First is pilot compatibility, for a gundam so long as someone is willing, physical able and has a decent amount of brain power they can potentially become a pilot while only a very small percent of the human population can ever be a candidate to pilot an eva. The second one is stability of piloting ability, for the gundam pilot so long as they train there ability to operate there suit will improve with the only factors that can effect this physical, nervous or mental damage, chemical instability or the pilots psychology while for a eva pilot it's true that experience builds ability and and so long as an eva pilot arms and brain are working, theoretically physical damage has less of an effect, eva pilots have an added complication in the fact that an eva pilot has an syncro ratio which is a measure of of how smoothly the eva links with the pilots mind. The problem with this is that no matter how much experience an eva pilot has there syncro ratio is the final arbitrator as to how effectively a pilot can operate and it can go up and down based on a pilots mood, in fact it can even drop low enough that operation is imposable(!) while a gundam pilot mood does play a part in how well they operate the only way they can lose compatibility at piloting (baring physical and or mental trauma and or damage) is by not operating a suit for a long period of time and the only way a they can drop to zero piloting ability is through extreme mental and or physical damage and or trauma. So in the end it seems that piloting an gundam is considerably easier then piloting an eva, well at lest in my opinion that is...

[]:             Quote Post by Taekmkm » Mon Oct 12, 2009 7:17 am Gundams are far harder to pilot. Normal humans (I.E. not newtypes like Amuro) have an extremely hard time using them to their maximum abilities, not counting the "almost" Gundams like Ground-Combat Gundam, Ez-08, etc. A good example would be Christina from 0080, a non-newtype that tied to a half-repaired Zaku (a relic at that point in the war) while using the most advanced Gundam at that point. Not to mention G-forces in space, and no super-protection like AT Fields on the battlefield. If you're a qualified pilot for EVA, it's pretty easy to get the hang of it quickly, since most of its mental. If you aren't a newtype/coordinator/enhanced human, you require a lot of training to even move a Gundam right.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 12, 2009 9:59 am Piloting an Evangelion is not determined by skill. So this discussion is therefore irrelevant and pointless. The minute this discussion devolves into an Eva vs. Gundam thread, it will be murdered with fire and rusty hammers.

[]:             Quote Post by Ryjin » Mon Oct 12, 2009 10:04 am @Taekmkm: Know that I think of it, wasn't a newtypes main perk outside of there ability to take high gees was a physic piloting ability (this is of course coming from my fuzzy memory of gundam..as for googling...lazy panda is lazy)? If so this pretty much means they are piloted the same way, joysticks for the common task with brainwaves caring out the more complex stuff. And another factor you reminded me of was the fact true gundams are designed for space combat, this fact would make gundams extremely complected to pilot as not only wold there be like ten million more doohickeys then a jet fighter you also have to deal with vectors (i.e true 3D combat) and you got to be damn near a polymath genius thinking 10 steps ahead to dogfight in a vacuum at high gees as the act of even turning directions in space requires a complex reassignment of inertia to the gundam. With this in mind I do Still think piloting an eva has it's fair share of issues but I have to retract what I said earlier, do to there space based operations gundams win the complex operation award. However I still believe that a eva would be harder to use if gundams where ground only machines.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Mon Oct 12, 2009 10:38 am The Eva Monkey wrote: Piloting an Evangelion is not determined by skill. So this discussion is therefore irrelevant and pointless. The minute this discussion devolves into an Eva vs. Gundam thread, it will be murdered with fire and rusty hammers.

So this discussion is therefore irrelevant and pointless. The minute this discussion devolves into an Eva vs. Gundam thread, it will be murdered with fire and rusty hammers.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 12, 2009 5:45 pm Dr. Nick wrote: The Eva Monkey wrote: Piloting an Evangelion is not determined by skill. So this discussion is therefore irrelevant and pointless.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Mon Oct 12, 2009 6:31 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: Even if skill is involved, the series does nothing to indicate it

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 12, 2009 6:48 pm Dr. Nick wrote: Shinji got swallowed by Leliel, Asuka didn't.

[]:             Quote Post by NemZ » Mon Oct 12, 2009 6:53 pm The Eva Monkey wrote: After only a few sorties, both Asuka and Shinji are doing complex acrobatics and an over-the-top rider-kick.

[]:             Quote Post by Dr. Nick » Mon Oct 12, 2009 7:02 pm It seems I view the concept of "piloting skill" in more encompassing terms than Monkey. For example, in my opinion the ability to make full use of that element of surprise is skill.

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Mon Oct 12, 2009 8:12 pm Dr. Nick wrote: It seems I view the concept of "piloting skill" in more encompassing terms than Monkey. For example, in my opinion the ability to make full use of that element of surprise is skill.

Now from this comparison it would seem that the eva and gundam are a pretty even match, with them both having analogue controls with the more complex operations being handled of screen, a data processor for a gundam and the pilots brain for the eva, though some may argue that the fact that instead of having to hit a button to move, an eva pilot only has to think to move may give then the edge, a counter point can be bought up that muscle memory, once a pilot has gain enough, reacts faster then one can think (but in practice this is probably a moot point however as it in the end comes down to personal preface with some finding it easier to think something out while other find developing muscle memory easier), which again equals them out, there are 2 things that work in the gundams favor if we look a little more closely. Article: Which is more difficult to pilot Eva or Gundam
 down to personal preface with some finding it easier to think something out while other find developing muscle memory easier), which again equals them out, there are 2 things that work in the gundams favor if we look a little more closely. First is pilot compatibility, for a gundam so long as someone is willing, physical able and has a decent amount of brain power they can potentially become a pilot while only a very small percent of the human population can ever be a candidate to pilot an eva. The second one is stability of piloting ability, for the gundam pilot so long as they train there ability to operate there suit will improve with the only factors that can effect this physical, nervous or mental damage, chemical instability or the pilots psychology while for a eva pilot it's true that experience builds ability and and so long as an eva pilot arms and brain are working, theoretically physical damage has less of an effect, eva pilots have an added complication in the fact that an eva pilot has an syncro ratio which is a measure of of how smoothly the eva links with the pilots mind. The problem with this is that no matter how much experience an eva pilot has there syncro ratio is the final arbitrator as to how effectively a pilot can operate and it can go up and down based on a pilots mood, in fact it can even drop low enough that operation is imposable(!) while a gundam pilot mood does play a part in how well they operate the only way they can lose compatibility at piloting (baring physical and or mental trauma and or damage) is by not operating a suit for a long period of time and the only way a they can drop to zero piloting ability is through extreme mental and or physical damage and or trauma. So in the end it seems that piloting an gundam is considerably easier then piloting an eva, well at lest in my opinion that is...
